title: centaurea jacea subsp. transalpina
intro: Centaurea jacea subsp. transalpina, also known as Transalpine knapweed or mountain hard-head, is a subspecies of perennial plant in the sunflower family, characterized by its tall stem and clusters of small, yellow flowers. Traditionally, Centaurea jacea subsp. transalpina has been used in herbal medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which may help to soothe digestive issues and skin irritations. The plant's health benefits are attributed to its ability to reduce inflammation, promote wound healing, and exhibit antimicrobial activity, making it a potential remedy for various health conditions. In herbal preparations, Centaurea jacea subsp. transalpina is often used as a dried herb or infused in teas, tinctures, and salves to harness its medicinal properties.
